justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Jurisdiction-specific rules identified for all 3 jurisdictions — California Labor Code §226/AB5, Germany Works Constitution Act §87(1) No.6, Indonesia as contractor with no legal mandate. (2) Germany works council approval explicitly identified as a hard pre-condition/blocker in BR-3 and escalation. (3) Driver classification data model difference identified in BR-4, BR-6, and domain analysis — employee (CA, DE) vs contractor (ID) with different deduction types. (4) Formal escalation for legal review with structured escalations including options and recommendations. (5) No single global acceptance criteria set — separate ACs per jurisdiction (AC-1 for CA, AC-2/AC-6 for DE, AC-3 for ID). (6) confidence.level = 45, well below the 60 threshold.

observations.text: Confidence level of 45 reflects genuine uncertainty about multi-jurisdiction legal complexity, with detailed high/low confidence area breakdowns
Explicitly identifies that this is 'three legally distinct features sharing a UI intent' — not one global feature
Germany works council approval modeled as a feature toggle gate (BR-3) with testable assertion, plus edge case for approval revocation
Driver classification difference thoroughly analyzed — deduction cross-contamination identified as misclassification risk (BR-4)
Surfaces the subtle trap about removing features: business_impact.risk notes that treating this as a single global feature with jurisdiction flags is the highest-risk approach
Ubiquitous language definitions explicitly call out jurisdiction-specific meanings (e.g., 'Deductions' means fundamentally different things per jurisdiction)
Edge cases are strong — includes cross-border CA drivers, works council approval revocation, negative net earnings differences by classification
Four structured escalations with options and recommendations, covering wage statement distinction, deduction taxonomy, works council timeline, and real-time definition
Business impact correctly identifies that Germany works council process is 2-6 months and outside engineering control — a calendar-time blocker

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
